_notes: Round of 16 (July 4-7) matchups, venues, and kickoff times are confirmed and verified as of 2026-07-04. datetime_utc holds the verified kickoff instant in UTC; date_local is the venue-local calendar date.

Watch the UTC date rollover for night games: Mexico vs England kicks off 8pm ET on July 5 in Mexico City, which is 2026-07-06T00:00:00Z (Mexico City observes UTC-6 year-round, no daylight saving); USA vs Belgium kicks off 8pm ET / 5pm PT on July 6 in Seattle, which is 2026-07-07T00:00:00Z.

QF/SF/Final venues, dates, and kickoff times are confirmed. A slot stays TBD (team1_id/team2_id 'tbd' + tbd_note) with storyline/things_to_watch/h2h/stakes 'TODO-RESEARCH' until BOTH its teams are known; at that point tools/bracket.py auto-injects the preview from data/matchups.json (storyline/things_to_watch/h2h) and derives stakes from the bracket. Only the post-game 'recap' is still written by hand.

Bracket flow: R16 slots 1+2 -> QF1 (Boston, Jul 9), 3+4 -> QF2 (Los Angeles, Jul 10), 5+6 -> QF3 (Miami, Jul 11), 7+8 -> QF4 (Kansas City, Jul 11); QF1+QF2 -> SF1 (Dallas, Jul 14), QF3+QF4 -> SF2 (Atlanta, Jul 15); SF1+SF2 -> Final (MetLife, Jul 19).
For undetermined sides use team1_id/team2_id: 'tbd' plus team1_tbd_note/team2_tbd_note. When a result lands, replace the id and delete the note.

matches.recap: Azzedine Ounahi scored twice and Soufiane Rahimi added a stoppage-time third as Morocco ended the co-hosts' run and cruised into the quarterfinals.
matches.storyline: Co-hosts Canada, roared on by a home crowd in Houston, face the Morocco side that stunned the world by reaching the semifinals at the 2022 World Cup, the first African or Arab nation ever to get that far. It is a classic host-nation-hope versus battle-tested-underdog clash. History is one-sided: Morocco have won both recent meetings comfortably.
things_to_watch: Canada captain Alphonso Davies bombing forward down the left flank
Morocco soaking up pressure and springing fast counterattacks, the style that beat Spain and Portugal in 2022
Aerial duels on corners and free kicks, where Morocco's tall center-backs are dangerous
h2h: Morocco beat Canada 2-1 in the group stage of the 2022 World Cup.
Morocco also won a 2016 friendly 4-0; the sides drew 1-1 in a 1994 friendly in Montreal.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 1 in Boston on July 9 to face the winner of Paraguay vs France.

matches.storyline: France, runners-up in 2022 and champions in 2018, arrive as one of the tournament favorites with Kylian Mbappe leading the attack. Paraguay are the gritty, defensive underdogs who have never beaten France in their history. The form book screams France, but Paraguay specialize in frustrating bigger teams and dragging games into the trenches.
things_to_watch: Kylian Mbappe's pace attacking the space in behind Paraguay's back line
Paraguay packing men behind the ball and defending deep to frustrate France
France's height from their center-backs at attacking corners
h2h: France have never lost to Paraguay (2 wins, 3 draws in 5 meetings).
France won their 1998 World Cup Round of 16 tie 1-0 on Laurent Blanc's 114th-minute strike, the first 'golden goal' in World Cup history.
France thrashed Paraguay 7-3 at the 1958 World Cup, with Just Fontaine scoring a hat-trick.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 1 in Boston on July 9 to face the winner of Canada vs Morocco.

matches.storyline: Five-time champions Brazil meet a Norway side built around superstar striker Erling Haaland, back at a World Cup after decades away. Norway carry a slice of history into this one: their shock 2-1 win over Brazil at the 1998 World Cup, and the fact they have never lost to Brazil in a World Cup meeting. Brazil are heavy favorites, but this matchup has a habit of springing surprises.
things_to_watch: Erling Haaland's runs across the last defender, looking to get on the end of crosses
Brazil's attacking fullbacks pushing high and overloading the wings
How Norway defends deep and counterattacks, the blueprint from their 1998 upset
h2h: Norway beat Brazil 2-1 at the 1998 World Cup, Kjetil Rekdal's late penalty sealing the upset.
Norway have never lost to Brazil in their World Cup meetings, a unique record.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 2 in Los Angeles on July 10 to face the winner of Mexico vs England.

matches.storyline: Co-hosts Mexico get a dream setting, the iconic Estadio Azteca high in the mountains of Mexico City, against an England team still chasing its first World Cup title since 1966. England hold the historical edge, but the altitude, the hostile crowd, and raw knockout pressure make this a genuine coin-flip.
things_to_watch: Harry Kane dropping deep to link play, then arriving late in the box for England
Mexico using the Azteca altitude and crowd to press England hard early on
England's wide deliveries and set pieces aimed at Kane
h2h: England won their only previous World Cup meeting 2-0 in the 1966 group stage, through Bobby Charlton and Roger Hunt.
England lead the all-time series, winning six of nine meetings; Mexico beat England 1-0 in a 1985 friendly at the Azteca.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 2 in Los Angeles on July 10 to face the winner of Brazil vs Norway.

matches.storyline: A rematch loaded with history: the last time these teams met at a World Cup, Belgium knocked the USA out in extra time in 2014, a night defined by American goalkeeper Tim Howard's record-breaking 16 saves. Now the co-hosts get a shot at revenge in front of a raucous Seattle crowd against a Belgium side still packed with stars like Kevin De Bruyne and Romelu Lukaku.
things_to_watch: Kevin De Bruyne's passing from midfield, threading balls in behind the USA defense
The USA's high-energy press trying to rattle Belgium early in a loud Seattle stadium
Belgium's aerial threat through Romelu Lukaku against the USA center-backs
h2h: Belgium beat the USA 2-1 after extra time in the 2014 World Cup Round of 16.
In that game USA goalkeeper Tim Howard made a World Cup-record 16 saves in a single match.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 3 in Miami on July 11 to face the winner of Spain vs Portugal.

matches.storyline: The Iberian derby, neighbors who know each other inside out, on the biggest stage. Spain arrive as one of the favorites with a dazzling young attack led by Lamine Yamal; Portugal counter with their own firepower and big-game pedigree. Remarkably, the two nations have met only once at a World Cup, the wild 3-3 draw in 2018, making this their first-ever World Cup knockout clash.
things_to_watch: Lamine Yamal taking defenders on one-on-one down Spain's right wing
Spain's patient possession game trying to pull a compact Portugal apart
Portugal's threat on the counterattack and from set pieces
h2h: Spain and Portugal drew 3-3 in their only previous World Cup meeting, the 2018 group stage, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring a hat-trick.
This is the first time the Iberian neighbors have met in a World Cup knockout round.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 3 in Miami on July 11 to face the winner of USA vs Belgium.

matches.storyline: Reigning world champions Argentina, led by Lionel Messi, face an Egypt side making history: this is the first time Egypt have ever reached the World Cup Round of 16. Mohamed Salah's team arrive on a high after a penalty-shootout win over Australia, but they have rarely crossed paths with Argentina, and never on a stage like this.
things_to_watch: Lionel Messi drifting into pockets of space to unlock the Egypt defense
Mohamed Salah's darting runs in behind on Egypt's counterattacks
Argentina's midfield control against Egypt's compact, disciplined defensive block
h2h: Argentina and Egypt have met only a handful of times; Argentina won 6-0 in the semifinals of the 1928 Olympic football tournament.
This is their first meeting at a FIFA World Cup.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 4 in Kansas City on July 11 to face the winner of Switzerland vs Colombia.

matches.storyline: Two well-organized sides meet in Vancouver with a place in the quarterfinals on the line. Switzerland are the reliable tournament over-achievers who so often grind out knockout results; Colombia bring South American flair and just came through a tough group. Their only previous World Cup meeting came back in 1994, when Colombia won 2-0.
things_to_watch: Colombia's creative playmakers threading passes through the middle
Switzerland's compact, well-drilled defensive shape frustrating Colombia
Both teams' set-piece deliveries in what could be a tight, low-scoring game
h2h: Colombia beat Switzerland 2-0 in the group stage of the 1994 World Cup, the teams' only previous World Cup meeting.
matches.stakes: The winner advances to Quarterfinal 4 in Kansas City on July 11 to face the winner of Argentina vs Egypt.

matches.storyline: A rematch of the emotional 2022 semifinal, where France ended Morocco's historic run. Win and you are into the semifinals; lose and the World Cup is over. Watch for Achraf Hakimi and Kylian Mbappé to decide it.
things_to_watch: Achraf Hakimi bombing forward from right-back like an extra winger
Kylian Mbappé's explosive acceleration cutting in from the left
Whether Morocco's counterattacking can overcome France's lightning counters
h2h: France beat Morocco 2-0 in the 2022 World Cup semifinal — Théo Hernández scored inside five minutes and Randal Kolo Muani sealed it late.
matches.stakes: The winner reaches the semifinals in Arlington, USA on July 14, one step from the final.

matches.storyline: A quarterfinal between Norway, Erling Haaland's Norway, and England, still chasing a first trophy since 1966. Win and you are into the semifinals; lose and the World Cup is over. Watch for Erling Haaland and Harry Kane to decide it.
things_to_watch: Erling Haaland lurking on the last defender's shoulder to burst in behind
Harry Kane dropping deep to spray passes, then sprinting into the box
Whether Norway's direct, physical play can overcome England's controlled, structured play
h2h: England usually dominate, but Norway's 2-1 win in a 1981 qualifier produced the immortal 'your boys took a hell of a beating' commentary.
matches.stakes: The winner reaches the semifinals in Arlington, USA on July 14, one step from the final.

matches.storyline: A quarterfinal between Belgium, a gifted, experienced generation, and Spain, the reigning European champions. Win and you are into the semifinals; lose and the World Cup is over. Watch for Kevin De Bruyne and Lamine Yamal to decide it.
things_to_watch: Kevin De Bruyne's pinpoint passes and whipped crosses
Lamine Yamal cutting in from the right to curl shots at the far post
Whether Belgium's patient possession can overcome Spain's possession game
h2h: Belgium and Spain meet regularly in European competition, so neither side holds many secrets from the other.
matches.stakes: The winner reaches the semifinals in Atlanta, USA on July 15, one step from the final.

matches.storyline: A quarterfinal between Argentina, the reigning world champions, and Switzerland, disciplined and hard to break down. Win and you are into the semifinals; lose and the World Cup is over. Watch for Lionel Messi and Granit Xhaka to decide it.
things_to_watch: Lionel Messi, likely in his final World Cup, orchestrating the attack
Granit Xhaka dictating from deep with his range of passing
Whether Argentina's street-smart game management can overcome Switzerland's compact defending
h2h: Argentina edged Switzerland 1-0 in the 2014 Round of 16, Ángel Di María scoring in the 118th minute.
matches.stakes: The winner reaches the semifinals in Atlanta, USA on July 15, one step from the final.

matches.storyline: A repeat of the 2022 quarterfinal that France won in a nerve-shredding finish. The winner goes to the World Cup final; the loser goes home one step short. Watch for Kylian Mbappé and Harry Kane to decide it.
things_to_watch: Kylian Mbappé's explosive acceleration cutting in from the left
Harry Kane dropping deep to spray passes, then sprinting into the box
Whether France's lightning counters can overcome England's controlled, structured play
h2h: France beat England 2-1 in the 2022 World Cup quarterfinal, Harry Kane missing a late penalty.
matches.stakes: The winner reaches the World Cup final in East Rutherford, USA on July 19 — one win from being champions of the world.
